channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [KREONFIN] Kreon Finnancial Services Ltd Promoters' Regulation 31(4) Disclosure for FY 2025-2026
item.description:
Kreon Finnancial Services Ltd has received the annual disclosure from Mr. Jaijash Tatia, Promoter of the Company, on behalf of Promoter and Promoter Group of the Company under Regulation 31(4) of the SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011 for the financial year ended March 31, 2026. The Promoters, Mr. Jaijash Tatia and the Promoter Group, collectively held 11242169 equity shares of Kreon Finnancial Services Limited as on March 31, 2026, with no encumbrance or pledge on the shares during the financial year.

  • Kreon Finnancial Services Ltd promoters' annual disclosure received for FY 2025-2026.
  • Mr. Jaijash Tatia, Promoter, submitted the disclosure on behalf of the Promoter and Promoter Group.
  • Promoters collectively held 11242169 equity shares as on March 31, 2026.
  • No encumbrance or pledge was created, invoked, or released on the shares during the financial year.
  • Disclosure submitted under Regulation 31(4) of the SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: KREON FINNANCIAL SERVICES LTD. | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [SAMKRG] Samkrg Pistons & Rings Ltd.: Promoters Did Not Encumber Shares in FY 2025-2026
item.description:
In accordance with Regulation 31(4) of SEBI (SAST) Regulations, 2011, Samkrg Pistons & Rings Ltd. has submitted an annual disclosure stating that promoters did not create any encumbrance on their shareholding during the financial year ending March 31, 2026. The company has shared the yearly disclosure under regulation 31(a) of SEBI (substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011 for the year ended on March 31, 2026, for stakeholders' perusal and record.

  • Promoters of Samkrg Pistons & Rings Ltd. did not encumber their shares during the financial year 2025-2026.
  • The company has submitted the yearly disclosure under regulation 31(a) of SEBI (substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011.
  • The disclosure has been submitted for informing that the promoters have not made any encumbrance of their shareholding during the year.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SAMKRG PISTONS & RINGS LTD.-$ | Category: Monthly Update

item.title: [ESCORTS] Escorts Kubota Ltd. to Increase Tractor Prices from April 15, 2026
item.description:
Escorts Kubota Limited, a leading engineering conglomerate in India, has announced a price increase for its tractors other than the Kubota brand, effective April 15, 2026. The price increase will vary across models, variants, and geographies. The company, which has a rich history of eight decades in manufacturing excellence, is committed to creating value for its customers and shareholders through engineering innovation, market-relevant technologies, and cost efficiencies.

  • Escorts Kubota Limited to increase tractor prices from April 15, 2026
  • Price increase will vary across models, variants, and geographies
  • Company has a rich history of eight decades in manufacturing excellence
  • Committed to creating value for customers and shareholders through engineering innovation
  • Focus on market-relevant technologies and cost efficiencies

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Escorts Kubota Ltd | Category: Deals

item.title: [SATIN] Satin Creditcare Subsidiary SFL Achieves AUM Milestone, Strong Disbursement Growth in FY26
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Sa$n Finserv Limited (SFL), a wholly owned subsidiary of Sa$n Creditcare Network Limited, has reported a strong FY26 with significant growth in Assets Under Management (AUM) and disbursements. SFL crossed Rs. 1,000 crores+ AUM, marking a 93% YoY growth, and achieved Rs. 700 crores+ disbursements, reflecting a robust 120% YoY growth. The year also saw capital strengthening with Rs. 730 crores raised through debt and Rs. 90 crores equity infusion from the parent. SFL added 70+ new branches and expanded its employee base to 1,200+. The company will continue to focus on sustainable, calibrated growth, underpinned by portfolio quality, prudent risk management, and operational efficiency.

  • Crossed Rs. 1,000 crores+ AUM, marking a 93% YoY growth
  • Achieved Rs. 700 crores+ disbursements, reflecting a robust 120% YoY growth
  • Raised ~Rs. 730 crores through debt and secured Rs. 90 crores equity infusion from parent
  • Added 70+ new branches during the year
  • Expanded employee base to 1,200+ employees

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Satin Creditcare Network Ltd | Category: Quarterly Updates

item.title: [BAJAJ-AUTO] Bajaj Auto Ltd Announces Strong Sales for March 2026
item.description:
Bajaj Auto Ltd has released its sales figures for March 2026. The company reported a significant increase in sales, demonstrating a positive business performance. This information was shared in a press release as per the SEBI regulations.

  • Bajaj Auto Ltd reports strong sales for March 2026
  • The company's sales performance is a positive indicator of its business health

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BAJAJ AUTO LTD. | Category: Monthly Update

item.title: [BANKBARODA] Bank of Baroda Reports 13.93% YoY Growth in Global Business in March 2026
item.description:
Bank of Baroda has reported a YoY growth of 13.93% in its global business, reaching ₹ 30.79 trillion as of 31st March 2026. The bank's global advances grew by 16.23% to ₹ 14.30 trillion, and global deposits increased by 12.00% to ₹ 16.49 trillion during the same period. The growth was consistent across various segments, including domestic deposits and advances.

  • Global Business grew by 13.93% YoY to ₹ 30.79 trillion
  • Global Advances grew by 16.23% YoY to ₹ 14.30 trillion
  • Global Deposits grew by 12.00% YoY to ₹ 16.49 trillion
  • Domestic Deposits grew by 12.83% YoY to ₹ 14,01,497
  • Domestic Advances grew by 14.56% YoY to ₹ 11,69,804

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BANK OF BARODA | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [DELHIVERY] Delhivery Ltd Grants 58,250 Stock Options Under ESOP 2012 to Eligible Employees
item.description:
Delhivery Ltd, in accordance with Regulation 30 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, has approved the grant of 58,250 stock options under Delhivery Employees Stock Option Plan 2012 (ESOP 2012) to eligible employees. The options, each convertible into one fully paid-up equity share with a face value of Re. 1/-, will vest in stages over a period of 4 years from the date of grant. The exercise price is set at Re. 1/- per share.

  • Grant of 58,250 stock options under ESOP 2012 to eligible employees
  • Each stock option is convertible into one fully paid-up equity share with a face value of Re. 1/-
  • Options to vest in stages over a period of 4 years from the date of grant
  • Exercise price set at Re. 1/- per share
  • Delhivery Employees Stock Option Plan 2012 in compliance with SEBI regulations

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Delhivery Ltd | Category: Investment

item.title: [HEXT] Hexaware Technologies Ltd: Director Changes, AGM, and Committee Reconstitution
item.description:
Hexaware Technologies Ltd announced that its Board, at a meeting held on April 02, 2026, approved several decisions. The 33rd Annual General Meeting (AGM) of the members of the Company will be held on Tuesday, May 05, 2026, through video conferencing/other audio-visual means. Mr. Milind Sarwate, Independent Director, will cease to be a Director of the Company upon completion of his second consecutive term. Mr. Alok Chandra Misra has been appointed as his successor. The Board also approved the reconstitution of Committees, with Mr. Alok Chandra Misra and Ms. Sukanya Kripalu appointed as Chairpersons of several Committees.

  • 33rd Annual General Meeting (AGM) on May 05, 2026
  • Mr. Milind Sarwate ceasing to be a Director
  • Appointment of Mr. Alok Chandra Misra as Independent Director
  • Reconstitution of Committees of the Board
  • Appointment of Ms. Sukanya Kripalu as Chairperson of the Nomination & Remuneration Committee

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Hexaware Technologies Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [HEXT] Hexaware Technologies Ltd: AGM Date Fixed, Director Milind Sarwate to Cease Membership & Committees Reconstituted
item.description:
Hexaware Technologies Ltd has fixed the date of its 33rd Annual General Meeting (AGM) on May 05, 2026, through video conferencing/other audio-visual means. The company also announced that Mr. Milind Sarwate, Independent Director, will cease to be a Director of the Company on April 24, 2026, upon completion of his second consecutive term. Mr. Alok Chandra Misra has been appointed as his successor. The Board has also approved the reconstitution of committees, with Mr. Alok Chandra Misra appointed as the Chairman of the Audit Committee and Environmental, Social & Governance Committee, and Ms. Sukanya Kripalu as the Chairperson of the Nomination & Remuneration Committee.

  • AGM date fixed for May 05, 2026
  • Director Milind Sarwate to cease membership on April 24, 2026
  • Alok Chandra Misra appointed as successor to Milind Sarwate
  • Reconstitution of Audit Committee and Environmental, Social & Governance Committee
  • Sukanya Kripalu appointed as Chairperson of the Nomination & Remuneration Committee

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Hexaware Technologies Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [PANELEC] Pan Electronics India Ltd Repays Loan from Punjab National Bank
item.description:
Pan Electronics India Ltd has repaid the loan amount obtained from Punjab National Bank. The original loan amount was Three Crore, which was modified to Rupees 71 Lakhs Five thousand Only on 18.07.2025. The loan was fully repaid on 02-04-2026. The loan was secured by a charge on entire Current Assets such as Stock, Book Debts and Raw Materials for Cash Credit, and Hypothecation of Machineries Purchased for Term Loan. The repayment of this loan is not expected to have a material impact on the company's financial or operational aspects.

  • Pan Electronics India Ltd repaid loan from Punjab National Bank
  • Original loan amount was Three Crore, modified to Rupees 71 Lakhs Five thousand Only
  • Loan was secured by a charge on Current Assets and Hypothecation of Machineries
  • Loan was fully repaid on 02-04-2026
  • Repayment of loan is not expected to have a material impact on the company's financial or operational aspects

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: PAN ELECTRONICS INDIA LTD. | Category: Legal
